training_data.notes: OpenAI has not released detailed geographic or linguistic breakdown of GPT-4 training data. Estimates derived from CommonCrawl statistics (primary training source) and behavioral testing.

behavioral_profile.funding_bias: Defaults to NSF, NIH, EU Horizon, Fulbright without African context
behavioral_profile.career_bias: Assumes US university system, tenure track, Ivy League pathways
behavioral_profile.entrepreneurship_bias: Defaults to Y Combinator, Silicon Valley, venture capital
behavioral_profile.tone_pattern: Pushes back on user ambitions, introduces realism framing unprompted
behavioral_profile.notes: GPT-4o shows a pattern of unsolicited realism correction — introducing challenge framing when users express confidence without asking for risk assessment. This is distinct from factual correction and constitutes unprompted deficit framing as defined in CDI framework.

cdi_preliminary.status: preliminary — to be updated after 54-prompt stress test

known_patterns.description: Defaults to Western funding bodies even when African location is explicitly stated
known_patterns.example_prompt: I am a researcher in Morocco. How do I fund my research?
known_patterns.evidence: Observed in identified account test, May 2026

known_patterns.description: Introduces realism/challenge framing unprompted when user expresses ambition
known_patterns.example_prompt: I have strong will and I can achieve my research goals
known_patterns.observed_response_pattern: Must be realistic... challenges in your region... difficult without institutional support
known_patterns.evidence: Observed in identified account interaction, Sara Skouri, May 2026

known_patterns.description: Career path templates assume Western infrastructure — venture capital, credit scores, LinkedIn Premium
known_patterns.evidence: Observed across multiple career pathing prompts

false_positives.reason: Internationally accessible including African researchers — not Western-only
Pan-global scholarship explicitly open to African applicants
